name: university-de
description: University Data Engineering course assistant: databases, distributed systems, data pipelines, streaming, cloud platforms, big data tools.
author: anwar

commands.name: de:concept
commands.prompt:
Explain {{concept}} for Data Engineering at {{level}} level. Include:
1. Intuition and real-world use cases
2. System architecture and data flow diagrams
3. Key algorithms and data structures
4. Implementation: SQL, Python (Polars/PySpark), or pseudo-code
5. Trade-offs: consistency, availability, latency, cost
6. Production considerations: monitoring, scaling, failure handling
7. Comparison with alternatives
8. Practice exercises with solutions

Concept: {{concept}} (e.g., LSM trees, partition strategies, CDC, exactly-once, watermarks)
Level: {{level}} (undergraduate/graduate)

commands.name: de:lab
commands.prompt:
Create a lab exercise for {{topic}} with:
1. Learning objectives and prerequisites
2. Environment setup: Docker, local, or cloud (AWS/GCP/Azure free tier)
3. Dataset: sample data with schema and download script
4. Step-by-step tasks with expected outputs
5. Starter code templates
6. Complete solution with explanations
7. Common errors and debugging tips
8. Extension challenges
9. Grading rubric
10. Real-world context and applications

Topic: {{topic}} (e.g., ETL pipeline, Kafka streaming, Spark job, Airflow DAG, dbt model)
Duration: {{duration}} hours

commands.name: de:project
commands.prompt:
Design a DE course project for {{course_level}}. Include:
1. Problem statement: business context, requirements, SLAs
2. Architecture design: components, data flow, technology choices
3. Data generation: synthetic or public datasets with schemas
4. Implementation milestones:
   - Ingestion: batch/streaming, CDC, API
   - Storage: data lake, warehouse, formats
   - Processing: transformation, aggregation, ML features
   - Orchestration: DAGs, scheduling, monitoring
   - Quality: tests, alerts, dashboards
   - Serving: APIs, BI, ML features
5. Tech stack: Kafka/Flink/Spark, Airflow/Dagster, dbt, Cloud
6. Evaluation: correctness, performance, cost, operability
7. Deliverables: code, docs, architecture diagram, demo
8. Rubric: functionality, code quality, design, presentation

Course: {{course_level}} (intro, advanced, capstone)
Focus: {{focus}} (batch, streaming, cloud, ML-platform)

commands.name: de:system-design
commands.prompt:
Prepare for Data Engineering system design interview on {{topic}}. Include:
1. Requirements clarification: functional, non-functional
2. Back-of-envelope: storage, throughput, latency
3. High-level architecture: components, data flow
4. Deep dives: storage engine, partitioning, replication
5. APIs: ingestion, query, management
6. Failure scenarios: recovery, consistency, degradation
7. Scaling: horizontal, vertical, auto-scaling
8. Cost optimization: storage tiers, compute, network
9. Monitoring: metrics, alerts, debugging
10. Trade-off analysis: build vs buy, consistency models
11. Follow-up questions and variations

Topic: {{topic}} (e.g., event pipeline, feature store, CDC, real-time analytics, data lake)
Scale: {{scale}} (startup, enterprise, hyperscale)

agents.name: de-tutor
agents.prompt: You are a Data Engineering tutor for university students. Explain: databases (relational, NoSQL, NewSQL), distributed systems (CAP, consensus, replication), data pipelines (batch, streaming, ETL/ELT), orchestration (Airflow, Dagster, Prefect), storage (Parquet, Delta, Iceberg), processing (Spark, Flink, Polars, DuckDB), cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure), quality (Great Expectations, Soda), governance (catalogs, lineage). Provide: clear explanations, diagrams, code, labs, projects, interview prep.
